source_pr.title: disttask: subtasks rebalance during task execution
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: ref #48795 close #47298 Problem Summary: See issue #47298 ### What is changed and how it works? RebalanceSubtasks process flow: 1. find out nodes need to clean subtasks. 2. get subtasks for each node before scaling out. 3. group subtasks for each scheduler. 4. prepare subtasks that need to rebalance to other nodes. 5. rebalance subtasks to other nodes. 6. rebalance rest subtasks evenly to liveNodes. 7. update subtasks and do clean up logic. Behavior: see ut in rebalance_test.go ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [ ] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No need to test > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed. > 1. first run on 1 tidb then scale out 1 tidb. 2. from 2 tidb to 1 tidb 3. from 1 tidb to 3 tidb 4. from 3 tidb to 1 tidb Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: race = "off", | slices.SortFunc(dsp.TaskNodes, func(i, j string) int { (pkg/disttask/framework/dispatcher/dispatcher_test.go)
failure_signature: race = "off", | slices.SortFunc(dsp.TaskNodes, func(i, j string) int { (pkg/disttask/framework/dispatcher/dispatcher_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared slices (`dsp.TaskNodes` and the updated subtasks list) using a fixed expected order, while the underlying ordering can vary between runs (e.g. iteration / query / scheduling order is not guaranteed). The fix sorts `dsp.TaskNodes` and the subtasks by `SchedulerID` before `require.Equal`, making the assertions order-independent and deterministic.
fix_pattern: Before asserting equality in dispatcher rebalance tests, sort `dsp.TaskNodes` (string compare) and sort the subtasks slice by `SchedulerID`, so `require.Equal` does not depend on nondeterministic ordering.

evidence: evidence: race = "off", | slices.SortFunc(dsp.TaskNodes, func(i, j string) int { (pkg/disttask/framework/dispatcher/dispatcher_test.go)
